factors.summary: The company has a significant backlog of aircraft orders, indicating strong demand.

evidence.text: As of March 31, 2015, we have firm orders to purchase 81 aircraft. We also have options to acquire 46 B737 aircraft with deliveries from 2018 through 2024 and have options to acquire five Q400 aircraft with deliveries from 2018 through 2019.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its capital expenditure plans, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: Our capital expenditures were $279 million in the first three months of 2015, primarily due to purchase deposits related to 34 aircraft deliveries over the next 24 months.

factors.summary: The company is generating strong free cash flow, supporting its operations and capital expenditures.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $514 million during the first three months of 2015, compared to $242 million during the same period in 2014.

factors.summary: The company is actively returning capital to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.

evidence.text: In addition, we continued to return capital to our shareholders by paying $26 million in quarterly dividends and repurchasing $102 million of our common stock.

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging, indicating a stronger balance sheet.

evidence.text: Long-term debt, net of current portion decreased by 5.2% from $686 million to $650 million.

factors.summary: The economic environment is favorable, with strong revenue growth and stable credit ratings.

evidence.text: In 2014, we were rated BBB- by Fitch Ratings and Standard & Poor's, and are now one of only two U.S. airlines with investment-grade credit ratings.

factors.summary: Demand for summer travel is strong, supported by low fuel costs and customer value initiatives.

evidence.text: Currently, we see strong demand for the summer travel season. Because of our low cost structure, low fuel costs, and our strong balance sheet we are able to offer lower fares, while maintaining our return and capital allocation objectives.

factors.summary: The company is successfully offering lower fares due to low fuel costs, which is enhancing customer value without significant pricing power.

evidence.text: Because of our low cost structure, low fuel costs, and our strong balance sheet we are able to offer lower fares, while maintaining our return and capital allocation objectives.

factors.summary: Fuel costs are significantly lower, decreasing by 40.4% per gallon year-over-year, providing cost relief.

evidence.text: The average price of raw fuel decreased 38.9% per gallon, and non-fuel unit expenses decreased 0.6%.

factors.summary: Revenue passenger miles increased by 9.1%, driven by capacity expansion and slight yield decline, indicating growing demand.

evidence.text: Revenue passenger miles (RPM) (000,000) 'traffic' 7,723   7,078   9.1% Available seat miles (ASM) (000,000) 'capacity' 9,257   8,352   10.8%

factors.summary: Business and leisure travel is mixed, with competitive markets affecting yields, but leisure-focused promotions are enhancing demand.

evidence.text: The decrease in yield and load factor was primarily due to increased competitive capacity in our markets and, to a lesser extent, new markets that have not yet fully matured. [...] We also launched a promotion beginning February 1, 2015 to offer a free checked bag to all Alaska Airlines Visa Signature credit cardholders to further enhance the value of our Mileage Plan program, which we expect to continue.

factors.summary: Load factor declined slightly, but capacity is increasing, with CASM excluding fuel showing slight improvement.

evidence.text: Load factor 83.4%   84.7%   (1.3) pts [...] CASM excluding fuel 8.61¢   8.66¢   (0.6%)

factors.summary: The company is expanding its route network with new non-stop routes, signaling a strategic focus on growth and market penetration.

evidence.text: New Non-Stop Routes Launched in Q1 New Non-Stop Routes Announced (Launch Dates) Las Vegas to Mammoth Lakes, California Seattle to JFK, New York (9/16/15) San Diego to Kona, Hawaii Orange County, California to Cabo San Lucas, Mexico (10/8/15) Seattle to Washington, D.C. Dulles Orange County, California to Puerto Vallarta, Mexico (10/9/15) Boise to Spokane, Washington (8/24/15) Los Angeles to Baltimore, Maryland (9/10/15) Seattle to Nashville, Tennessee (9/23/15) Seattle to Raleigh, North Carolina (10/1/15) Seattle to Charleston, South Carolina (11/16/15) Los Angeles to San Jose, Costa Rica (10/31/15) Los Angeles to Liberia, Costa Rica (11/1/15)

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its fleet capacity, aligning with its growth strategy, and expects a 10.5% increase in capacity in the second quarter, signaling confidence in demand growth.

evidence.text: The table below reflects our full-year expectation for capital expenditures and the additional expenditures if options were exercised. These options will be exercised only if we believe return on invested capital targets can be met.
We expect our capacity to increase approximately 10.5% in the second quarter. [...] Our current expectations for capacity and CASM excluding fuel and special items are summarized below:

factors.summary: The company's fuel cost management is mixed. While they are effectively capping crude oil costs through call options, there are mark-to-market hedge losses that have negatively impacted their financials.

evidence.text: All of our current oil positions are call options, which are designed to effectively cap the cost of the crude oil component of our jet fuel purchases. With call options, we benefit from a decline in crude oil prices, as there is no cash outlay other than the premiums we pay to enter into the contracts.
We recognized losses of $6 million for hedges that settled during the first quarter of 2015 , compared to losses of $18 million in the first quarter of 2014 .

factors.summary: The company's margins are stable and improving, driven by strong financial management, efficient operations, lower fuel costs, and cost management efforts. Despite the overall positive trend, the evidence highlights specific areas contributing to margin expansion, such as decreased non-fuel unit costs and reduced operating expenses.

evidence.text: Given our strong financial condition, we will continue to evaluate our cash flows from operations, reinvest in the business, and return capital to our shareholders, while maintaining a strong liquidity position.
Total operating expenses decreased $50 million , or 5% , compared to the first quarter of 2014. [...] Non-fuel expenses 796   723   10 Total Operating Expenses $ 1,031   $ 1,081   (5 ).
